original_abstract: This article is dedicated to Prof. George Lotter, a pivotal figure in the development of more than 90 postgraduate students in Practical Theology at North-West University (NWU). Under his mentorship, there has been a significant surge in empirical research, reflecting a broader global shift toward evidence-based inquiry within the discipline. As Practical Theology increasingly engages with complex societal issues, the development of robust empirical research capacities among both lecturers and students has become essential. This article identifies a specific need for more rigorous methodology during the qualitative analysis phase of research. By evaluating current research outputs at NWU and synthesizing established qualitative traditions, the author argues for a more systematic approach to data coding. The article culminates in a proposed five-phase model designed to enhance the credibility and depth of data analysis in practical theological studies.
humanized_abstract: This article is dedicated to Prof. George Lotter who supervised more than 90 postgraduate students in Practical Theology at the North-West University (NWU). Recently it seems that empirical research in Practical Theology is emerging worldwide, which indicates the need for developing students, lecturers and academics on empirical research skills. While Practical Theology is involved with a variety of societal problems, the ability to do empirical research in a reliable way is a huge challenge. This article tries to identify one of the challenges in empirical research and especially in qualitative research: the methodology of analysing empirical data. It explores the qualitative research methodologies, which are used by postgraduate students at NWU, and relates it to the qualitative research traditions found in the literature. As a result, a way to address this challenge, which can enhance the quality of empirical research in particular in the qualitative way of analysing empirical data in Practical Theology, is proposed. The article attempts to establish a 5 phase model to address empirical data analysis in practical theological research.
